response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack software engineer with strong web, backend, and developer-tooling experience, but the role requires deep hardware-oriented expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in the resume. His low-latency DLang systems work and brief C/C++ IDE tooling experience at National Instruments are the closest touchpoints, but they fall well short of principal-level performance architecture for AI silicon.
strengths: Long, consistent engineering career with senior/staff roles at major companies (Google, Meta)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang, async I/O, millions of requests/sec)
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling (MCP, agentic frameworks)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or GPU/accelerator workload analysis background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No DL workload performance optimization background
Strong general seniority and systems performance fundamentals
